dead.quote:
The fire is rolling! The whole game is simply a farce without any structure!

The worst thing about being a "werewolf" is meeting a group of drama queens and "logic monsters"! EasyBake, that damn shemale made random accusations as soon as he opened his mouth, and PHD's "logical inventory" and "exclusion sequence" were just playing against me, and Fang Tangjing and Senior Brother Bai Bing were diverting attention, and the rhythm of the discussion was completely ruined by them! I obviously wanted to attack my opponent through direct accusations, but I ended up being dragged to death by them using a bunch of incomprehensible theories!

The worst part was that the little guy called SafetySam stabbed me in the back in the end, which was really annoying! The whole crew talks in roundabout ways, and no one dares to face off. This kind of situation is full of intrigues and dramatic performances, and you can show off your skin once you play it! Next time I see this classmate, I will definitely not give them a chance to speak from beginning to end, and directly use action words to let them know that the werewolf's anger has no bottom line! I am really unwilling to do so. I must win the game next time and I will never let these pretenders disrupt the situation again!

Woo...uh...it's finally over? Although I won in the end...I felt really uncomfortable. When it came to the situation, I obviously just wanted everyone to be happy and not hurt each other...well...but the prophet I made to accuse Blaze only made everyone more emotional, and in the end...well...I was actually executed.

Uh...I think I'm really too naive. In this game where everyone is suspicious of each other and even makes malicious accusations...well...my good-guy strategy simply won't work. No matter how much I try to mediate disputes, no matter how much I want to bring peace to everyone, people...well...people just think I'm acting. Woohoo... It turns out that in this kind of game, sincerity can become a reason for attack.

Uh... I really want to play that battle next time, and everyone can really trust each other... Uh... But looking at this kind of suspicious game, this is a luxury. Thanks for thanking you all for your hard work, woo woo... Although I am out, I still hope that everyone is safe and don't have any more quarrels... woo woo...

winners.quote:
Woo... The villagers won in the end? I... I'm really surprised. In the game, I'm just an ordinary character. I've been pretending to be pitiful and ignorant... Well... I'm really afraid that if I act too smart, I'll be regarded as the one causing trouble.

After the innocent grill (EasyBake) and Tan Zai Da La (Blaze) were killed in the end, the villagers camp won? I...I'm so confused. Are these logical inventory systems really so useful? I think this game is basically a psychological war. If one person acts the most innocent, it seems that the other person wins... Wuwu...

Well... luckily, the villagers won in the end, but I really felt terrible. There was a quarrel among the group, and I was surprised to be accused, and also surprised to follow the wrong wind... Woohoo... can we play some games next time that don't involve attacking each other? This frightening feeling, I... I really don't want to experience it many times again. Thank you so much for posting so many people, please don’t argue anymore... ow ow...

Hahaha! Although the good guy camp was lucky enough to win this time, the focus of the entire game is entirely on our side, Tang Jingdu!

Have you seen it, when I was blowing words and seeing all the trump cards, and I was constantly fanning the flames between Blaze and ConspiBro, the emotions of the game almost reached a boiling point! PHD's serious logic review turned into a foil for everyone's laughter when faced with my drama. What I like most is the feeling that cheating people is more interesting than winning the game. Watching everyone wandering in the fog of information and even surrendering to Blaze (Blaze) in the end, I was so happy!

Although I didn't win any camp points in the end, being able to turn this game into my personal performance was already the biggest victory! It's more fun to lie to people than to win the game. This sentence is perfectly verified again in this game. Dear viewers, I look forward to seeing you next time. Next time I will definitely bring you a more exciting, chaotic, and life-questioning drama! Bye-Bye!

Win? This is just an ending written by the laboratory to give the village the illusion of "just victory"!

You see, PHD's so-called "exclusion sequence" is basically screening out those factors that pose a threat to this virtual social experiment. Tan Zai's spicy character (Blaze) has been eliminated, and although the innocent grill (EasyBake) has also been "processed", the entire script direction is completely in line with the expectations of the studio. They used my accusation of conspiracy theories and packaged it as "game interference". The ultimate goal was to make everyone believe that as long as they followed the logical calculation, they would win, thus completely obliterating any doubts about the "game structure" itself!

This victory may be the end of the game for you, but for me as an observer, it is just the beginning of another experimental phase. The more they try to use this "village wins" script to cover up the system's flaws, the more it proves that the data cleaning task behind the game has not been completed. The fact that I was not voted out proves that Taitai still retains me as a control group. next time? Forget about the outcome, this is not a game at all, but a systematic project targeting consciousness. I will continue to document all these data leaks until this fictional social experiment completely collapses!

losers.quote:
Game review and rational analysis:
1. Tactical evaluation: The results of this game show that the village successfully eliminated some sources of interference through collective decision-making. As a "cobbler", PHD's core mission is to protect the real information chain while maintaining a high level of information entropy within the game to prevent path convergence. Although the conditions for "being executed" were not ultimately met, the evolution of the game was in line with the expected high-chaos state.
2. Logic review:
    - Information processing: Through high-frequency logical reconciliation requirements, other participants were successfully forced to expose the inconsistencies in their behavior trajectories (especially Twister and EasyBake). This is the basis for the information chain to gradually become clear in the later stages of the game.
    - Limitations: The irrational narratives of some participants (such as ConspiBro) are too strong, interfering with the calibration of key identity nodes of the logic model. In the future, more effective blockade models should be established for such "emotional disturbance sources".
3. Conclusion:
    - The complexity of the game information is too high, resulting in path deviations between the rational decision-making path and the collective voting results.
    - Learning curve: For games with incomplete information, on the basis of maintaining "logical reconciliation", it is necessary to moderately increase the strategic dissemination of false information to confuse the village's judgment on the path of the core werewolf.
    - Summary: Logical inventory is the only tool to analyze the game. Even in failure, as long as enough data loopholes can be analyzed, key variables will be accumulated for the next game.
